## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On July 2, 1964, United States President Lyndon B. Johnson officially and aggressively signed the massive, incredibly monumental Civil Rights Act of 1964 into federal law. Following the massive, heavily televised brutalities of the Birmingham campaign and the absolute, unrelenting pressure of the Civil Rights Movement, this incredibly historic legislation aggressively outlawed massive discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin, fundamentally and heavily ending absolute, systemic Jim Crow segregation in public accommodations across the entire nation.

On July 2, 1881, US President James A. Garfield was violently and aggressively assassinated in Washington, D.C. Charles J. Guiteau, a massively delusional and heavily disgruntled office seeker who falsely believed he was personally responsible for Garfield's massive electoral victory, heavily shot the President at the Baltimore and Potomac Railroad Station. Garfield's tragic, massive lingering death heavily catalyzed absolute, sweeping civil service reform, culminating in the massive Pendleton Act to aggressively eliminate the deeply corrupt "spoils system."

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 2, 1972, the incredibly massive, highly historic, and deeply consequential Simla Agreement was officially and aggressively signed by Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and Pakistani President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto. Following the massive, absolute military defeat of Pakistan and the incredible creation of Bangladesh in the 1971 war, this massive diplomatic treaty aggressively committed both nations to heavily resolve their massive differences through peaceful, bilateral negotiations, fundamentally establishing the absolute framework for the modern Line of Control (LoC) in Kashmir.

On July 2, 1757, Siraj-ud-Daulah, the massive, highly powerful, and absolute final independent Nawab of Bengal, was brutally and aggressively assassinated in Murshidabad. Following his incredibly massive, catastrophic military defeat by the British East India Company at the Battle of Plassey just days prior, he was aggressively captured and heavily executed under the orders of Mir Jaran, the son of the traitorous Mir Jafar. His massive death officially and absolutely marked the terrifying beginning of direct, massive British colonial domination over the subcontinent.
